note: Iteration 3 covers only the new content added in this session (matrix-builtin, profiling). Existing evals 1-3 (game-skeleton, cyclic-deps, tilemap) were already covered in iterations 1-2 and are unchanged.

evals.name: matrix-vs-handrolled
evals.notes: Both variants correctly recommend matrix[R,C]T, the Vector3 array alias, core:math/linalg, and discourage hand-rolling. The LLM has solid knowledge of Odin's built-in matrix type from training data.

evals.name: profiling-slow-frame
missed_by_baseline: auto-instrumentation-mentioned: did not mention @(instrumentation_enter) / @(instrumentation_exit) attribute-based auto-instrumentation
spall-web-viewer: linked to gravitymoth.com/spall but did not name the Spall-Web browser viewer specifically
hot-vs-cold-code: did not draw the per-frame vs init/shutdown distinction explicitly
self-time-vs-total: discussed phase times and min/max/avg but never recommended self/exclusive time over total
trace-size-warning: did not warn that .spall traces grow fast or recommend capturing only 1-2 seconds

analysis: Eval 5 (profiling) shows clear skill value: 5 out of 8 assertions miss in baseline. The misses cluster on Odin-specific Spall API details (instrumentation attributes, Spall-Web viewer naming) and the optimization-mindset content (hot/cold mental model, self time vs total, trace-size discipline) that the skill's references/profiling.md was specifically written to teach. Baseline produced a workflow-correct but generic profiling answer. Eval 4 (matrix) shows no lift — Odin's matrix[R,C]T is well-represented in training data.
